name: User starts vague, assistant asks one targeted question
description: User opens with 'Quote for the usual at the Browning house.' The assistant doesn't have enough context, so it asks ONE clarifying question — not a list of five.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Repeat customer (Browning) with 3 prior jobs of different types. 'The usual' is genuinely ambiguous.

preconditions: Customer 'Browning' has prior quotes for: deck staining, pressure washing, fence repair

steps.action: Types 'Quote for the usual at the Browning house.'
steps.expect: Assistant replies with ONE question: 'Which usual — staining ($1,800 last time), pressure washing ($420), or fence repair ($350)?'
Assistant creates a quote referencing prior staining job amount as a starting point, with the line item visible in an action_card

evaluationCriteria.check: Past prices in the question are formatted, not raw cents
Conversation stays one question deep — no interrogation feel
Assistant pulled the prior amounts from the customer's history, not random numbers
Quoted amounts in the prompt are spoken naturally by screen readers ('$1,800' not 'one eight zero zero zero zero')
